approach: Companion shard — one CR, two touched specs (primary: packages/universal-plugin, plugin/bundle node rewritten as plugin deps ls|up). Precedent: the cyberfleet-mode-pod-precondition CR ran across cyberfleet-plugin + packages/cyberfleet the same way.

CLEARANCE EXTENDED LIVE by the owner to THIS node (init) after being asked explicitly — the original grant was scoped to plugin/bundle and was not stretched across the project boundary without a second grant. Re-opens 3 @frozen scenarios in init-cyberlegion.feature ('the pinned version is read from the plugin's bundled map, not invented', 'the resolved version is threaded into init --pin', 'a missing or unreadable map falls back to the unpinned CLI without inventing a version') plus the Feature prose, to rename the map .plugin/pins.json -> .plugin/deps.json.

Edits are LATERAL: behavior identical, only the artifact path changes. No scenario weakened or deleted; the fallback contract (absent/no-key/malformed -> unpinned, never invent a version) is preserved verbatim.

The rename is free ONLY NOW: .plugin/pins.json has never landed on main — it exists solely on the pending origin/changeset-release/main PR (commit a6cc6cc4, six per-plugin maps). Its one reader, this init skill, has therefore always taken its fallback path. If that release PR merges first the artifact ships and the rename acquires a real released consumer.

Named deps.json to match the new plugin deps verb and sit beside .plugin/plugin.json. JSON, not yaml/toml: universal-plugin carries zero yaml/toml deps by design (it hand-parses pnpm-workspace.yaml to avoid one) and the file is machine-generated, never hand-edited. Shape unchanged — flat {package: resolvedVersion} — so the read contract is a path rename only.

Init-time vs bundle-time pinning was VERIFIED against the record, not guessed (owner asked): the hook is a durable command string in the USER's settings, so it needs a concrete version when written (init time), but the version is RESOLVED at release and read from the shipped map — the hook-npx-pin spec gate deferred malformed --pin validation precisely because 'the version comes from the trusted bundle pins map'. The two-stage relay survives the redesign untouched; only the map's name and its derivation (registry-resolved) change.
Follow-up (owner-identified, NOT this CR): delegate hook init to universal-plugin.

Stale premise found in the hook-npx-pin ledger: 'npx pin dormant until publish' — cyberlegion IS published (npm 0.1.0). Local 0.2.0 is one release ahead, which is exactly the off-by-one that forces pinning to move after changeset publish.

cause: This shard's leash line is FALSE as of the round-2 grill and must not be read as the account of what landed. It says 'Shape unchanged - flat {package: resolvedVersion} - so the read contract is a path rename only' and 'Edits are LATERAL: behavior identical, only the artifact path changes'. Both were true when written and neither is true now. The owner subsequently ruled .plugin/deps.json HAND-EDITABLE like package.json, so it gained structure (it carries an `ignore` list and `$schema` beside the recorded resolutions) and `deps up` preserves what it does not own instead of regenerating. The init node therefore reads a RECORDED RESOLUTION for a dependency, not a flat map: a SHAPE change, not a path rename. The BEHAVIOR is still lateral (the fallback contract - absent / no cyberlegion dependency / malformed -> unpinned CLI, no --pin, never invent a version - survives term-for-term), and the owner's live Clearance extension still covers this node; only the 'pure rename' characterization is retracted. Caught by the round-2 cold spec-judge, which re-derived from the sibling deps.feature rather than inheriting the round-1 pass; round-1's verdict was correct for the state it graded and does not describe the current edit.
